The mitigation of risks associated with GenAI models is no
control and must encompass additional procedural check
initial risk assessment to implementation testing and final
Ongoing monitoring 
The model risk related to any model use needs to be mon
plan that includes: 
•   A reasonable monitoring frequency. 
•   A justified monitoring approach. 
•   Sufficient and clearly defined Key Performing Ind
•   Acceptable thresholds for the KPIs that can succe
•   A final decision-making guidance based on the m
•   Action plans for poor model performance. 
 A well-developed monitoring plan should reveal error pat
weakness.

query domain stability, etc. or operational KPIs relevant to
feedback, number of successful vs attempted generation,
human calibration might be required to alleviate costs of

•   Embed “Term of use” alerts in user 
interface for GenAI platforms.  
•   Include disclaimers within the generated 
text or alongside it. 
•   Use preprocessing to block harmful or 
unethical input prompts. 
•   Define a collection of pre-designed 
prompts, templates or examples curated 
to guide the behavior of GenAI  
•   Guardrails (typically additional models) to 
detect and minimize toxicity and 
hallucination risks. 
•   Set bounds on response length or 
complexity to avoid generating overly 
detailed or harmful outputs.
